HGSS.738.0001:
The foe is physically
pounded with a long
tail or a foreleg,
etc.
HGSS.738.0002:
The foe is attacked
with a sharp chop.
It has a high
critical-hit ratio.
HGSS.738.0003:
The foe is slapped
repeatedly, back
and forth, two to
five times in a row.
HGSS.738.0004:
The foe is hit with
a flurry of punches
that strike two to
five times in a row.
HGSS.738.0005:
The foe is slugged
by a punch thrown
with muscle-packed
power.
HGSS.738.0006:
Numerous coins are
hurled at the foe to
inflict damage.
Money is earned
after battle.
HGSS.738.0007:
The foe is punched
with a fiery fist.
It may leave the
target with a burn.
HGSS.738.0008:
The foe is punched
with an icy fist.
It may leave the
target frozen.
HGSS.738.0009:
The foe is punched
with an electrified
fist. It may leave
the target with
paralysis.
HGSS.738.0010:
Hard, pointed, and
sharp claws rake the
foe to inflict damage.
HGSS.738.0011:
Huge, impressive
pincers grip and
squeeze the foe.
HGSS.738.0012:
A vicious, tearing
attack with pincers.
The foe will faint
instantly if this
attack hits.
HGSS.738.0013:
A two-turn attack.
Blades of wind hit the
foe on the second
turn. It has a high
critical-hit ratio.
HGSS.738.0014:
A frenetic dance to
uplift the fighting
spirit. It sharply
raises the user’s
Attack stat.
HGSS.738.0015:
The foe is cut with a
scythe or a claw.
It can also be used
to cut down thin
trees.
HGSS.738.0016:
A gust of wind is
whipped up by wings
and launched at the
foe to inflict damage.
HGSS.738.0017:
The foe is struck
with large, imposing
wings spread wide
to inflict damage.
HGSS.738.0018:
The foe is blown away,
to be replaced by
another Pokémon
in its party. In the
wild, the battle ends.
HGSS.738.0019:
The user soars, then
strikes on the second
turn. It can also be
used for flying to
any familiar town.
HGSS.738.0020:
A long body or
tentacles are used
to bind and squeeze
the foe for two to
five turns.
HGSS.738.0021:
The foe is slammed
with a long tail,
vines, etc., to inflict
damage.
HGSS.738.0022:
The foe is struck
with slender, whiplike
vines to inflict
damage.
HGSS.738.0023:
The foe is stomped
with a big foot.
It may also make the
target flinch.
HGSS.738.0024:
The foe is quickly
kicked twice in
succession using both
feet.
HGSS.738.0025:
The foe is attacked
by a kick launched
with muscle-packed
power.
HGSS.738.0026:
The user jumps up
high, then strikes
with a kick. If the
kick misses, the
user hurts itself.
HGSS.738.0027:
The user lashes 
out with a quick,
spinning kick. It
may also make the
target flinch.
HGSS.738.0028:
Sand is hurled in the
foe’s face, reducing
its accuracy.
HGSS.738.0029:
The user attacks with
its head. It may make
the foe flinch. To find
Pokémon, hit trees.
HGSS.738.0030:
The foe is jabbed
with a sharply
pointed horn to
inflict damage.
HGSS.738.0031:
The foe is jabbed
repeatedly with a
horn or beak two to
five times in a row.
HGSS.738.0032:
The foe is stabbed
with a horn rotating
like a drill.
The foe instantly
faints if it hits.
HGSS.738.0033:
A physical attack
in which the user
charges and slams
into the foe with
its whole body.
HGSS.738.0034:
The user drops onto
the foe with its full
body weight.
It may leave the foe
paralyzed.
HGSS.738.0035:
A long body or vines
are used to wrap and
squeeze the foe for
two to five turns.
HGSS.738.0036:
A reckless, full-body
charge attack for
slamming into the foe.
It also damages the
user a little.
HGSS.738.0037:
The user rampages
and attacks for two
to three turns.
It then becomes
confused, however.
HGSS.738.0038:
A reckless, life-
risking tackle.
It also damages the
user by a fairly large
amount, however.
HGSS.738.0039:
The user wags its tail
cutely, making the
foe less wary.
The target’s Defense
stat is lowered.
HGSS.738.0040:
The foe is stabbed
with a poisonous
barb of some sort.
It may also poison
the target.
HGSS.738.0041:
The foe is stabbed
twice by a pair of
stingers.
It may also poison
the target.
HGSS.738.0042:
Sharp pins are shot
at the foe in rapid
succession.
They hit two to five
times in a row.
HGSS.738.0043:
The foe is given an
intimidating leer with
sharp eyes.
The target’s Defense
stat is reduced.
HGSS.738.0044:
The foe is bitten with
viciously sharp fangs.
It may make the
target flinch.
HGSS.738.0045:
The user growls in an
endearing way, making
the foe less wary.
The target’s Attack
stat is lowered.
HGSS.738.0046:
The foe is scared off,
to be replaced by
another Pokémon in
its party. In the
wild, the battle ends.
HGSS.738.0047:
A soothing lullaby is
sung in a calming
voice that puts the
foe into a deep
slumber.
HGSS.738.0048:
The user generates
odd sound waves from
its body.
It may confuse the
target.
HGSS.738.0049:
The foe is hit with a
destructive shock
wave that always
inflicts 20 HP damage.
HGSS.738.0050:
For several turns,
this move prevents
the foe from using
the move it last
used.
HGSS.738.0051:
The foe is attacked
with a spray of harsh
acid. It may also
lower the target’s
Sp. Def stat.
HGSS.738.0052:
The foe is attacked
with small flames.
The target may also
be left with a burn.
HGSS.738.0053:
The foe is scorched
with an intense blast
of fire. The target
may also be left with
a burn.
HGSS.738.0054:
The user cloaks its
body with a white mist
that prevents any of
its stats from being
cut for five turns.
HGSS.738.0055:
The foe is blasted
with a forceful shot
of water.
HGSS.738.0056:
The foe is blasted by
a huge volume of
water launched under
great pressure.
HGSS.738.0057:
It swamps the entire
battlefield with a
giant wave.
It can also be used
for crossing water.
HGSS.738.0058:
The foe is struck
with an icy-cold
beam of energy.
It may also freeze
the target solid.
HGSS.738.0059:
A howling blizzard is
summoned to strike
the foe.
It may also freeze
the target solid.
HGSS.738.0060:
The foe is attacked
with a peculiar ray.
It may also leave the
target confused.
HGSS.738.0061:
A spray of bubbles is
forcefully ejected
at the foe.
It may also lower the
target’s Speed stat.
HGSS.738.0062:
The foe is hit with a
rainbow-colored beam.
It may also lower the
target’s Attack
stat.
HGSS.738.0063:
The foe is attacked
with a powerful beam.
The user must rest
on the next turn to
regain its energy.
HGSS.738.0064:
The foe is jabbed
with a sharply
pointed beak or
horn.
HGSS.738.0065:
A corkscrewing
attack with the
sharp beak acting
as a drill.
HGSS.738.0066:
The user grabs the
foe and recklessly
dives for the ground.
It also hurts the
user slightly.
HGSS.738.0067:
A powerful low kick
that makes the foe
fall over. It inflicts
greater damage on
heavier foes.
HGSS.738.0068:
A retaliation move
that counters any
physical attack,
inflicting double the
damage taken.
HGSS.738.0069:
The foe is thrown
using the power of
gravity. It inflicts
damage equal to
the user’s level.
HGSS.738.0070:
The foe is slugged
with a punch thrown
at maximum power.
It can also be used
to move boulders.
HGSS.738.0071:
A nutrient-draining
attack. The user’s
HP is restored by
half the damage taken
by the target.
HGSS.738.0072:
A nutrient-draining
attack. The user’s
HP is restored by
half the damage taken
by the target.
HGSS.738.0073:
A seed is planted on
the foe. It steals
some HP from the foe
to heal the user on
every turn.
HGSS.738.0074:
The user’s body is
forced to grow all at
once. It raises the
Sp. Atk stat.
HGSS.738.0075:
Sharp-edged leaves
are launched to slash
at the foe. It has a
high critical-hit
ratio.
HGSS.738.0076:
A two-turn attack.
The user gathers
light, then blasts a
bundled beam on the
second turn.
HGSS.738.0077:
A cloud of poisonous
dust is scattered on
the foe.
It may poison the
target.
HGSS.738.0078:
The user scatters a
cloud of paralyzing
powder.
It may paralyze the
target.
HGSS.738.0079:
The user scatters a
big cloud of sleep-
inducing dust around
the foe.
HGSS.738.0080:
The user attacks by
scattering petals for
two to three turns.
The user then
becomes confused.
HGSS.738.0081:
The foe is bound with
silk blown from the
user’s mouth.
It reduces the
target’s Speed stat.
HGSS.738.0082:
The foe is stricken
by a shock wave.
This attack always
inflicts 40 HP
damage.
HGSS.738.0083:
The foe becomes
trapped within a
fierce vortex of fire
that rages for two

HGSS.738.0084:
A jolt of electricity
is hurled at the foe
to inflict damage.
It may also leave the
foe paralyzed.
HGSS.738.0085:
A strong electric
blast is loosed at
the foe.
It may also leave the
foe paralyzed.
HGSS.738.0086:
A weak electric
charge is launched at
the foe.
It causes paralysis if
it hits.
HGSS.738.0087:
A wicked thunderbolt
is dropped on the foe
to inflict damage.
It may also leave the
target paralyzed.
HGSS.738.0088:
The user picks up
and throws a small
rock at the foe to
attack.
HGSS.738.0089:
The user sets off an
earthquake that hits
all the Pokémon in
the battle.
HGSS.738.0090:
The user opens up a
fissure in the ground

The target instantly

HGSS.738.0091:
The user burrows,
then attacks on the
second turn.
It can also be used
to exit dungeons.
HGSS.738.0092:
A move that leaves
the target badly
poisoned.
Its poison damage
worsens every turn.
HGSS.738.0093:
The foe is hit by a
weak telekinetic
force. It may also
leave the foe
confused.
HGSS.738.0094:
The foe is hit by a
strong telekinetic
force. It may also
reduce the foe’s
Sp. Def stat.
HGSS.738.0095:
The user employs
hypnotic suggestion
to make the target
fall into a deep
sleep.
HGSS.738.0096:
The user meditates
to awaken the power
deep within its body
and raise its Attack
stat.
HGSS.738.0097:
The user relaxes and
lightens its body to
move faster.
It sharply boosts
the Speed stat.
HGSS.738.0098:
The user lunges at
the foe at a speed
that makes it almost
invisible. It is sure
to strike first.
HGSS.738.0099:
While this move is in
use, it gains attack
power each time the
user is hit in battle.
HGSS.738.0100:
Use it to flee from
any wild Pokémon.
It can also warp to
the last Pokémon
Center visited.
HGSS.738.0101:
The user makes the
foe see a mirage.
It inflicts damage
matching the user’s
level.
HGSS.738.0102:
The user copies the
move last used by
the foe. The move can
be used for the rest
of the battle.
HGSS.738.0103:
An earsplitting
screech is emitted to
sharply reduce the
foe’s Defense stat.
HGSS.738.0104:
By moving rapidly, the
user makes illusory
copies of itself to
raise its evasiveness.
HGSS.738.0105:
A self-healing move.
The user restores its
own HP by up to half
of its max HP.
HGSS.738.0106:
The user stiffens all
the muscles in its
body to raise its
Defense stat.
HGSS.738.0107:
The user compresses
its body to make
itself look smaller.
The user’s evasion
stat is boosted.
HGSS.738.0108:
The user releases an
obscuring cloud of
smoke or ink.
It reduces the foe’s
accuracy.
HGSS.738.0109:
The foe is exposed
to a sinister ray
that triggers
confusion.
HGSS.738.0110:
The user withdraws
its body into its hard
shell, raising its
Defense stat.
HGSS.738.0111:
The user curls up to
conceal weak spots
and raise its Defense
stat.
HGSS.738.0112:
The user throws up a
sturdy wall that
sharply raises its
Defense stat.
HGSS.738.0113:
A wondrous wall of
light is put up to
suppress damage from
special attacks for
five turns.
HGSS.738.0114:
The user creates a
haze that eliminates
every stat change
among all the Pokémon
engaged in battle.
HGSS.738.0115:
A wondrous wall of
light is put up to
suppress damage from
physical attacks for
five turns.
HGSS.738.0116:
The user takes a
deep breath and
focuses to raise the
critical-hit ratio of
its attacks.
HGSS.738.0117:
The user endures
attacks for two

HGSS.738.0118:
The user waggles a
finger and stimulates
the brain into

any move.
HGSS.738.0119:
The user counters
the foe by mimicking
the move last used
by the foe.
HGSS.738.0120:
The user blows up to
inflict damage on all
Pokémon in battle.
The user faints upon
using this move.
HGSS.738.0121:
A large egg is hurled
with maximum force at
the foe to inflict
damage.
HGSS.738.0122:
The foe is licked with
a long tongue, causing
damage.
It may also paralyze
the target.
HGSS.738.0123:
The foe is attacked
with a discharge of
filthy gases.
It may also poison
the target.
HGSS.738.0124:
Unsanitary sludge is
hurled at the foe.
It may also poison
the target.
HGSS.738.0125:
The user clubs the
foe with a bone.
It may also make the
target flinch.
HGSS.738.0126:
The foe is attacked
with an intense blast

It may also leave the
target with a burn.
HGSS.738.0127:
The user charges at
the foe rapidly, and
may make it flinch.
It can also be used
to climb a waterfall.
HGSS.738.0128:
The foe is clamped
and squeezed by

and sturdy shell for
two to five turns.
HGSS.738.0129:
Star-shaped rays are
shot at the foe.
This attack never
misses.
HGSS.738.0130:
The user tucks in its
head to raise its
Defense in the first
turn, then rams the
foe on the next turn.
HGSS.738.0131:
Sharp spikes are
fired at the foe to
strike two to five
times in rapid
succession.
HGSS.738.0132:
The foe is attacked
with long, creeping
tentacles or vines.
It may also lower the
target’s Speed.
HGSS.738.0133:
The user temporarily
empties its mind to
forget its concerns.
It sharply raises the
user’s Sp. Def stat.
HGSS.738.0134:
The user distracts
the foe by bending a
spoon.
It may lower the
target’s accuracy.
HGSS.738.0135:
The user restores its
own HP by up to half
of its maximum HP.
It may also be used
to heal an ally’s HP.
HGSS.738.0136:
The foe is attacked
with a knee kick from
a jump. If it misses,
the user is hurt
instead.
HGSS.738.0137:
The user intimidates
the foe with the
pattern on its belly
to cause paralysis.
HGSS.738.0138:
An attack that works
only on a sleeping
foe. It absorbs half
the damage caused to
heal the user’s HP.
HGSS.738.0139:
A cloud of poison gas
is sprayed in the
foe’s face.
It may poison the
target.
HGSS.738.0140:
Round objects are
hurled at the foe to
strike two to five
times in a row.
HGSS.738.0141:
A blood-draining
attack. The user’s
HP is restored by
half the damage taken
by the target.
HGSS.738.0142:
With a scary face, the
user tries to force a
kiss on the foe. If it
succeeds, the target
falls asleep.
HGSS.738.0143:
A second-turn attack
move with a high
critical-hit ratio.
It may also make the
target flinch.
HGSS.738.0144:
The user transforms
into a copy of the
foe right down to
having the same move
set.
HGSS.738.0145:
A spray of countless
bubbles is jetted at
the foe.
It may also lower the
target’s Speed stat.
HGSS.738.0146:
The foe is hit with
rhythmically launched
punches that may also
leave it confused.
HGSS.738.0147:
The user scatters
bursts of spores
that induce sleep.
HGSS.738.0148:
The user flashes a
light that cuts the
foe’s accuracy.
It can also be used

HGSS.738.0149:
The foe is attacked
with an odd, hot
energy wave.
The attack varies in
intensity.
HGSS.738.0150:
The user just flops
and splashes around
to no effect at all...
HGSS.738.0151:
The user alters its
cellular structure to
liquefy itself,
sharply raising its
Defense stat.
HGSS.738.0152:
The foe is hammered
with a large pincer.
This move has a high
critical-hit ratio.
HGSS.738.0153:
The user explodes to
inflict damage on all
Pokémon in battle.
The user faints upon
using this move.

The foe is raked with
sharp claws or
scythes for two to
five times in quick
succession.
HGSS.738.0155:
The user throws the
bone it holds. The
bone loops to hit the
foe twice, coming and

HGSS.738.0156:
The user goes to
sleep for two turns.
It fully restores the
user’s HP and heals
any status problem.
HGSS.738.0157:
Large boulders are
hurled at the foe to
inflict damage.
It may also make the
target flinch.
HGSS.738.0158:
The user bites hard
on the foe with its
sharp front fangs.
It may also make the
target flinch.
HGSS.738.0159:
The user reduces its
polygon count to
make itself more
jagged, raising the
Attack stat.
HGSS.738.0160:
The user changes its
type to become the
same type as one of

HGSS.738.0161:
The user strikes with
a simultaneous three-
beam attack. May also
paralyze, burn, or

HGSS.738.0162:
The user chomps hard
on the foe with its
sharp front fangs.
It cuts the target’s

HGSS.738.0163:
The foe is attacked
with a slash of
claws, etc.
It has a high
critical-hit ratio.
HGSS.738.0164:
The user makes a
copy of itself using
some of its HP.
The copy serves as
the user’s decoy.
HGSS.738.0165:
An attack that is
used in desperation
only if the user has
no PP. It also hurts
the user slightly.

It enables the user

the move last used by

Sketch disappears.
HGSS.738.0167:
A consecutive three-
kick attack that
becomes more
powerful with each 
successive hit.
HGSS.738.0168:
The user attacks and
steals the foe’s held
item simultaneously.
It can’t steal if the

HGSS.738.0169:
The user ensnares
the foe with a thin,
gooey silk so it can’t

The user senses the
foe’s movements

A sleeping foe is
shown a nightmare

damage every turn.
HGSS.738.0172:
The user cloaks

charges at the foe.
It may also leave the
target with a burn.

An attack that can
be used only if the

harsh noise may also

A move that works
differently for the
Ghost type than for

The user flails about

The user changes its
type to make itself

A vortex of air is
shot at the foe to
inflict damage.

The user releases
cottonlike spores
that cling to the
foe, sharply reducing
its Speed stat.

An all-out attack
that becomes more
powerful the less

the target.

The user throws a
punch at blinding

The user frightens

stat.
HGSS.738.0185:
The user draws up to

punch.

The user kisses the
foe with a sweet,

The user maximizes

the target.

The user hurls mud in
the foe’s face to
inflict damage and
lower its accuracy.
HGSS.738.0190:
The user attacks by
spraying ink in the
foe’s face or eyes.

The user fires an
electric blast like a

hit a Ghost type with

also enables the user

When this move is
used, if the user
faints, the foe that

Any Pokémon that
hears this song

The user attacks with
a gust of chilled air.

target’s Speed stat.

to evade all attacks.

rises if it is used in
succession.

The user strikes at
the foe with a hard
bone two to five

The user rampages
and attacks for two

turn.
HGSS.738.0393:
The user levitates
using electrically
generated magnetism
for five turns.
HGSS.738.0394:
The user cloaks
itself in fire and
charges at the foe.
The user sustains
serious damage, too.
HGSS.738.0395:
The foe is attacked
with a shock wave.
It may also leave the
target paralyzed.
HGSS.738.0396:
The user looses a
blast of aura power
from deep within its
body. This move is
certain to hit.
HGSS.738.0397:
The user polishes its
body to reduce drag.
It can sharply raise
the Speed stat.
HGSS.738.0398:
The foe is stabbed
with a tentacle or
arm steeped in
poison. It may also
poison the foe.
HGSS.738.0399:
The user releases a
horrible aura imbued
with dark thoughts.
It may also make the
target flinch.
HGSS.738.0400:
The user slashes the
foe the instant an
opportunity arises.
It has a high
critical-hit ratio.
HGSS.738.0401:
The user attacks by
swinging its tail as if
it were a vicious wave
in a raging storm.
HGSS.738.0402:
The user slams a
barrage of hard-
shelled seeds
down on the foe
from above.
HGSS.738.0403:
The user attacks with
a blade of air that
slices even the sky.
It may also make the
target flinch.
HGSS.738.0404:
The user slashes at
the foe by crossing
its scythes or claws
as if they were a pair
of scissors.
HGSS.738.0405:
The user vibrates its
wings to generate a
damaging sound wave.
It may also lower the
foe’s Sp. Def stat.
HGSS.738.0406:
The foe is attacked
with a shock wave
generated by the
user’s gaping mouth.
HGSS.738.0407:
The user tackles the
foe while exhibiting
overwhelming menace.
It may also make the
target flinch.
HGSS.738.0408:
The user attacks with
a ray of light that
sparkles as if it were
made of gemstones.
HGSS.738.0409:
An energy-draining
punch. The user’s
HP is restored by
half the damage taken
by the target.
HGSS.738.0410:
The user whirls its
fists to send a wave
of pure vacuum at
the foe. This move
always goes first.
HGSS.738.0411:
The user heightens
its mental focus and
unleashes its power.
It may also lower the
target’s Sp. Def.
HGSS.738.0412:
The user draws power
from nature and
fires it at the foe.
It may also lower the
target’s Sp. Def.
HGSS.738.0413:
The user tucks in
its wings and charges
from a low altitude.
The user also takes
serious damage.
HGSS.738.0414:
The user makes the
ground under the foe
erupt with power.
It may also lower the
target’s Sp. Def.
HGSS.738.0415:
The user trades held
items with the foe
faster than the eye
can follow.
HGSS.738.0416:
The user charges at
the foe using every
bit of its power.
The user must rest
on the next turn.
HGSS.738.0417:
The user stimulates
its brain by thinking
bad thoughts.
It sharply raises the
user’s Sp. Atk.
HGSS.738.0418:
The user strikes
with a tough punch as
fast as a bullet.
This move always goes
first.
HGSS.738.0419:
An attack move that
inflicts double the
damage if the user
has been hurt by the
foe in the same turn.
HGSS.738.0420:
The user flash
freezes chunks of
ice and hurls them.
This move always goes
first.
HGSS.738.0421:
The user slashes with
a sharp claw made
from shadows.
It has a high
critical-hit ratio.
HGSS.738.0422:
The user bites with
electrified fangs.
It may also make the
foe flinch or become
paralyzed.
HGSS.738.0423:
The user bites with
cold-infused fangs.
It may also make the
foe flinch or freeze.
HGSS.738.0424:
The user bites with
flame-cloaked fangs.
It may also make the
foe flinch or sustain
a burn.
HGSS.738.0425:
The user extends its
shadow and attacks
the foe from behind.
This move always goes
first.
HGSS.738.0426:
The user launches a
hard-packed mud ball
to attack.
It may also lower the
target’s accuracy.
HGSS.738.0427:
The user tears at
the foe with blades
formed by psychic
power. It has a high
critical-hit ratio.
HGSS.738.0428:
The user focuses its
willpower to its head
and rams the foe.
It may also make the
target flinch.
HGSS.738.0429:
The user looses a
flash of energy from
its polished body.
It may also lower the
target’s accuracy.
HGSS.738.0430:
The user gathers all
its light energy and
releases it at once.
It may also lower the
foe’s Sp. Def stat.
HGSS.738.0431:
A charging attack
that may also leave
the foe confused.
It can also be used
to scale rocky walls.
HGSS.738.0432:
It reduces the foe’s
evasion stat. It also
removes spikes,
light screens, etc.
HGSS.738.0433:
The user creates a
bizarre area in which
slower Pokémon get
to move first for five
turns.
HGSS.738.0434:
Comets are summoned
down from the sky.
The attack’s recoil
sharply reduces the
user’s Sp. Atk stat.
HGSS.738.0435:
A flare of electricity
is loosed to strike
all Pokémon in battle.
It may also cause
paralysis.
HGSS.738.0436:
An inferno of scarlet
flames washes over
all Pokémon in battle.
It may also inflict
burns.
HGSS.738.0437:
A storm of sharp
leaves is whipped up.
The attack’s recoil
sharply reduces the
user’s Sp. Atk stat.
HGSS.738.0438:
The user violently
whirls its vines or
tentacles to harshly
lash the foe.
HGSS.738.0439:
The user launches a
huge boulder at the
foe to attack.
It must rest on the
next turn, however.
HGSS.738.0440:
A slashing attack
that may also leave
the target poisoned.
It has a high
critical-hit ratio.
HGSS.738.0441:
The user shoots
filthy garbage at the
foe to attack.
It may also poison
the target.
HGSS.738.0442:
The foe slams the
target with its
steel-hard head.
It may also make the
target flinch.
HGSS.738.0443:
The user launches a
steel bomb that
sticks to the target.
This attack will not
miss.
HGSS.738.0444:
The user stabs the
foe with a sharpened
stone. It has a high
critical-hit ratio.
HGSS.738.0445:
If it is the opposite
gender of the user,
the foe is charmed
into sharply lowering
its Sp. Atk stat.
HGSS.738.0446:
The user lays a trap
of levitating stones
around the foe. The
trap hurts foes that
switch into battle.
HGSS.738.0447:
The user snares the
foe with grass and
trips it. The heavier
the foe, the greater
the damage.
HGSS.738.0448:
The user attacks
using a sound wave
based on words it has
learned. It may also
confuse the foe.
HGSS.738.0449:
The user releases
countless shots of
light. Its type varies
with the kind of Plate
the user is holding.
HGSS.738.0450:
The user bites the
foe. If the foe is
holding a Berry, the
user eats it and
gains its effect.
HGSS.738.0451:
The user fires a
concentrated bundle
of electricity. It may
also raise the user’s
Sp. Atk stat.
HGSS.738.0452:
The user slams its
rugged body into the
foe to attack. The
user also sustains
serious damage.
HGSS.738.0453:
The user lunges at
the foe at a speed
that makes it almost
invisible. It is sure
to strike first.
HGSS.738.0454:
The user calls out
its underlings to
pummel the foe.
It has a high
critical-hit ratio.
HGSS.738.0455:
The user calls out
its underlings to
shield its body,
raising its Defense
and Sp. Def stats.
HGSS.738.0456:
The user calls out
its underlings to heal
it. The user regains
up to half of its
max HP.
HGSS.738.0457:
The user delivers a
life-endangering
attack at full power.
The user also takes
terrible damage.
HGSS.738.0458:
The user slams the
foe with a long tail,
vines, etc. The
target is hit
twice in a row.
HGSS.738.0459:
The user blasts the
foe with power that
distorts even time.
The user must rest
on the next turn.
HGSS.738.0460:
The user tears the
foe along with the
space around it.
This move has a high
critical-hit ratio.
HGSS.738.0461:
The user faints. In
return, the Pokémon
taking its place will
have its status and
HP fully restored.
HGSS.738.0462:
The foe is crushed
with great force.
The attack is more
powerful the more HP
the foe has left.
HGSS.738.0463:
The foe becomes
trapped within a
maelstrom of fire
that rages for two
to five turns.
HGSS.738.0464:
The foe is dragged
into a world of total
darkness that puts it
to sleep.
HGSS.738.0465:
The user generates
a shock wave from
within its body.
It may also lower the
target’s Sp. Def.
HGSS.738.0466:
The user creates a
gust of repulsive
wind. It may also
raise all the user’s
stats at once.
HGSS.738.0467:
The user disappears,
then strikes the foe
on the second turn.
It hits even if the
foe protects itself.
